summary: This cluster investigates the control of harmful algal blooms (HABs) through oxidative stress induction, specifically targeting the dinoflagellate *Cochlodinium polykrikoides*. The research demonstrates that the biocide sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl) causes dose-dependent growth inhibition, with an EC50 of 0.584 mg L⁻¹, by damaging photosynthetic machinery and triggering antioxidant enzyme activity as a cellular stress response. The findings highlight a shared focus on the physiological mechanisms of oxidative damage and pigment disruption as a strategy for bloom mitigation. The geographic and ecological scope centers on marine environments impacted by toxic dinoflagellate blooms.
